title: MARTY SUPREME
logline: A charismatic but reckless ping pong hustler in 1950s New York navigates love, ambition, and self-destruction as he chases table tennis glory while his personal life spirals out of control.
antagonistOrConflict: Marty's own self-destructive impulses, compulsive lying, and inability to commit—externalized through Milton (business rival), his fractured relationship with Rachel, and the consequences of his hustling lifestyle
tone: Frenetic, darkly comic, morally ambiguous—a character study with propulsive energy and mounting desperation
themes: Self-sabotage and the inability to escape one's own patterns
The cost of ambition and the American Dream
Identity and performance (on and off the table)
Love as both salvation and destruction
The gap between talent and character

actBreaks.description: Marty returns from Europe to find his mother has discovered his theft; the cop arrives to arrest him, forcing him to flee his old life entirely.

validation.ruling: Scene #48 shows mundane domestic tension between Marty and his mother—not an act break. Scene #51 (p53, 32%) ends Act 1: a uniformed cop arrests Marty for armed robbery while Murray reveals he called the police and stole Marty's money. This irreversible plot twist forces Marty into new, higher-stakes conflict (legal jeopardy, betrayal, financial ruin) and fundamentally changes the story's direction from 'returning champion' to 'desperate criminal defendant.'

actBreaks.description: After the disastrous confrontation with Milton at the party and Kay's rejection, Marty hits absolute bottom and makes the desperate decision to go to Japan for one last shot at redemption.

validation.ruling: Scene #127 (p130, 77.4%) ends Act 2: Marty confesses to Kay that he stole her necklace intending to sell it, admits he's broke and has no way to get to Tokyo for the World Championship. This vulnerable confession and his admission of being 'completely full of shit' marks a crucial character turning point that propels him toward the final act's moral reckoning and the deal with Milton.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a pressure-cooker character study that accelerates relentlessly from scene 1 to scene 161. The structure is deceptively classical—three acts with clear breaks—but the pacing is pure anxiety. Act One (scenes 1-48) establishes Marty's dual life: shoe salesman by day, ping pong hustler and adulterer by night, culminating in his European tournament loss and return home to immediate consequences. Act Two (scenes 49-127) is an extended free-fall where every attempt Marty makes to stabilize his life—moving in with Dion, pursuing Kay, trying to launch the Globetrotters tour—collapses under the weight of his lies and impulsivity. The bathtub-through-the-floor sequence (scene 67) is the perfect visual metaphor for the entire act: structural collapse. Act Three (scenes 128-161) is a sprint to Japan and back, where Marty finally confronts what he's been running from—not Milton or Endo, but himself—and returns to find Rachel and their newborn child. The script refuses easy redemption; it ends on ambiguity, not triumph.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains breakneck momentum through rapid-fire scene work (average 1.4 pages per scene) and relentless geographic shifts. The European sequence (scenes 16-48) establishes Marty's talent and ego before the rug gets pulled. The middle section (scenes 49-113) is pure chaos—hotel disasters, car chases, confrontations—each scene raising stakes and tightening the noose. The Cranbury sequences (scenes 103-112, 142-150) provide brief rural respite before exploding into violence. The final Japan sequence (scenes 148-161) slows down just enough to let Marty's internal reckoning breathe before the quiet, devastating final image in the maternity ward.

incitingIncident.description: A uniformed cop arrests Marty for armed robbery in his apartment while Murray watches, revealing Murray called the cop and took money from Marty's luggage to settle a debt.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS

validation.ruling: Scene #22 is too early (12.5%) and merely shows Marty complaining about accommodations—a minor conflict that doesn't shatter his world. Scene #51 (p53, 32%) is the true inciting incident: Marty is arrested for armed robbery in his own apartment while Murray watches, revealing Murray called the cops and stole money from Marty's luggage. This irreversibly shatters Marty's status quo, forces immediate action, establishes the main conflict (legal/financial crisis), and introduces stakes that cannot be ignored.

midpoint.description: Marty enters the back office and finds Rachel with a suitcase and a black eye; she explains someone yelled at her, pushed her, and grabbed her shirt, and Marty embraces her.

validation.ruling: Scene #86 (Rachel arriving with a black eye) is a subplot development, not the protagonist's midpoint shift. Scene #98 (p98, 58%) is the true midpoint: Marty confronts Milton outside the Morosco Theater and agrees to throw the World Championship match in exchange for Milton paying his fine and legal fees. This is Marty's point of no return—he shifts from reactive survival to proactively making a Faustian bargain that raises personal stakes and fundamentally changes his trajectory toward Tokyo.

climax.description: With the score tied 17-17 and the amphitheater packed, Marty takes the lead 18-17, prompting Milton to approach and threaten to expose Marty's lies to Rachel unless he throws the match.

validation.ruling: Scene #155 (p162, 96.4%) is the climax: with the score tied 17-17 in front of a packed amphitheater, Milton confronts Marty mid-match and reminds him of their contract to throw the game. This is the peak confrontation where Marty must make his defining choice—honor the corrupt deal or fight for integrity—demonstrating his character arc completion at the moment of highest dramatic tension.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - STOCKROOM - LOWER EAST SIDE, NYC
scenes.summary: Marty Mauser dumps out a size 9 pair of shoes from a box in the stockroom and replaces them with a size 8, then carries the box upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - BACK AREA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty tries to force a size 8 shoe onto a customer's foot, claiming the brand runs small, then switches tactics and sells her a different brand by lying about its quality and longevity.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - BACK AREA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty silently gestures for a young woman to wait in the hallway while Murray spots him through the office window and calls him inside.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - MURRAY’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Murray gives Marty a business card promoting him to manager, but Marty rejects it, insisting he's not a shoe salesman and has bigger ambitions.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - BACK ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty beckons the young woman to duck past Murray's office window on her knees as they descend to the stockroom.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - STOCKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty and Rachel make out passionately in the stockroom, with Marty briefly pulling away when she mentions her husband has food poisoning, then they have sex against the shoeboxes.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty practices table tennis intensely with Wally at Lawrence's parlor while Lawrence coaches him and excited onlookers watch, then a phone rings and someone calls Marty to the booth.

scenes.heading: INT. PHONE BOOTH - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty answers the phone and Judy tells him his mother is very sick and asking for him, but Marty responds with indifference and suggests calling an ambulance instead.

scenes.heading: INT. JUDY’S APARTMENT - LOWER EAST SIDE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Judy whispers to Marty's mom that he's not buying the fake illness, then loudly claims into the phone that his mother just lost consciousness while Rachel walks past in the hallway carrying groceries.

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S APARTMENT - 2 FLOORS UP - MOMEN TS LATER
scenes.summary: Rachel rushes to her phone and eavesdrops on Judy's party-line conversation with Marty, then interrupts to tell Marty not to come home because his mother is faking.

scenes.heading: INT. HARDWARE STORE - MIDTOWN - SOON
scenes.summary: Dion keeps watch while Marty steals a can of spray paint from a hardware store and paints a ping pong ball orange.

scenes.heading: INT. GALANIS OFFICE - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty demonstrates to Galanis how an orange ball is easier to track than a white ball against white backgrounds, then pitches his branded ball idea using a magazine photo of a professional player.

scenes.heading: EXT. ORCHARD STREET - DUSK
scenes.summary: Marty runs across the street eating a hot dog and finds Norkin Shoes locked, while in voiceover he pitches Galanis on his upcoming tournament success and media coverage potential.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - MAIN FLOOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lloyd tells Marty that Murray left to take his mother to the hospital, Marty insists she's not sick and demands the money Murray owes him, then calls his mother who confirms Murray left for a trip to Kutsher's.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - BACK ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lloyd spins the safe shut when Marty enters, Marty pulls a gun from Murray's desk and demands the $700 Murray owes him, but Lloyd calls his bluff and Marty leaves without the money.

scenes.heading: EXT. 20,000 FEET IN THE AIR
scenes.summary: A Pan American propeller plane flies through the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - FIRST CLASS - DUSK
scenes.summary: Marty sips Coke in first class and looks out the window at a distant lightning storm.

scenes.heading: INT. LONDON CENTRAL HOSTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty arrives at a run-down London hostel, is recognized by other players, discovers he's sharing a cramped freezing room with four bunk beds, and learns the heat is coin-operated.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: All British Open competitors pose for a group photo, Marty notices three Japanese players and asks Béla about the travel ban being lifted, then stares at the poised Japanese team.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty defeats his first-round opponent Amit Vishwakarma with an impossible-to-return smash, then notices crowds have gathered around a table on the other side of the hall.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty plays with great flair in Round 3 against Vladimír Sebek and wins several points in a row.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - IATT OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty confronts Ram Sethi about the IATT housing star players at the Ritz while competitors stay in terrible conditions, demands better accommodations, and threatens to expose the disparity to the press.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty leverages his position and media attention to force the IATT to upgrade his accommodations, establishing his willingness to manipulate situations for personal gain.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty holds court with reporters at the Ritz, claims he's the only star player getting complimentary accommodations, dismisses Ted Bailey's loss and complaints about the Japanese player's sponge bat, and confidently predicts his own victory.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - ROYAL SUITE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Marty calls Kay from his lavish Royal Suite and invites her to breakfast, she initially declines mentioning her husband, but Marty persists with flirtatious banter until she agrees to come up.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - DOVER SUITE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kay sees Marty's newspaper interview, learns he's 23 and plays table tennis, while her husband Milton complains about his razor and bleeds from a shaving nick before leaving the room annoyed.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - LATER
scenes.summary: Kay arrives at the packed Wembley Stadium and watches Marty dominate his semifinal match against Béla Kletzki, winning at match point 20-9 with a powerful smash.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - D INING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty treats Béla to an expensive dinner, dismisses Béla's Globetrotters pitch, spots Kay dining with Milton and others, then instructs the maître d' to put the Rockwell table's bill on his room.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU - 1944 - DAY
scenes.summary: In a 1944 flashback, Béla sits by Auschwitz-Birkenau defusing bombs, finishes quickly to have free time, then notices bees and follows them into the woods.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - 1944 - SOON AFTER
scenes.summary: Béla blows cigarette smoke into a beehive to evacuate the bees, endures their stings stoically, breaks open the hive and smears honey all over his body.

scenes.heading: INT. AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU - BUNKER - 1944 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Béla lifts his shirt and prisoners crowd around him, greedily licking the honey off his naked chest and arms for nourishment.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - TOP FLOOR - MIDDLE OF NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kay exits the elevator, knocks on Marty's Royal Suite door, he answers in his underwear without glasses, and after a pregnant pause she enters with a feral look.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - ROYAL SUITE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kay and Marty have sex, with Marty staring at himself admiringly in the mirror while she climbs on top of him.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty loses the coin flip and Endo serves first in the finals, immediately dominating with blinding speed that Marty cannot return, losing the first five points in a row with increasing shock and embarrassment.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty's confident trajectory is shattered as he's completely outmatched by Endo's sponge bat technology in the finals, marking the beginning of his public humiliation.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty desperately smashes every return with maximum power to counter the sponge bat, wins two points this way, then snaps his fingers at an official while Milton and executives enter and take their seats.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty reverses strategy to soft hits but loses patience at match point, goes for a kill shot, sends it into the net, loses the match, then screams for a rematch and calls it bullshit while cameras flash.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty's public meltdown after losing the British Open finals destroys his reputation and marks his fall from rising star to disgraced competitor.

scenes.heading: EXT. 5TH ARRONDISSEMENT - PARIS - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty walks down a busy Paris street bordering the Seine and enters a restaurant.

scenes.heading: INT. LARUE - PARIS - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty approaches the maître d' at Larue and asks in broken communication to see Milton Rockwell's reservation.

scenes.heading: INT. LARUE - DINING ROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Milton shows Marty an expensive custom watch and lets him wear it, sends back his salad, then reveals he's incorporating table tennis into promotional events in Japan, while Marty burns his mouth on soup.
scenes.turningPointNote: Milton reveals his plan to commercialize table tennis in Asia, setting up the business rivalry and Marty's descent into the Globetrotters humiliation.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUDITORIUM - BOSNIA - EVENING
scenes.summary: Marty and Béla perform a slapstick halftime show for the Globetrotters in the rain in Sarajevo, playing 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star' with pots and pans to an unenthusiastic crowd.

scenes.heading: INT. AUDITORIUM - GREECE - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty and Béla play ping pong at a novelty miniature table on a basketball court floor in Athens.

scenes.heading: EXT. AMPHITHEATER - MOROCCO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty and Béla perform a choreographed multi-ball rally with five balls flying in looping arcs in Tangier.

scenes.heading: EXT. PYRAMIDS - EGYPT - DAY
scenes.summary: While the Globetrotters pose on camels for photos at the pyramids, Marty chisels away at a cornerstone with a pocketknife until a piece breaks free.

scenes.heading: INT. MID-SIZED ARENA - FRANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty plays ping pong against Maurice the sea lion in front of a packed Paris arena, deliberately loses the point, and forces a smile while feeling deep humiliation as the crowd erupts in laughter.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - LOWER EAST SIDE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Marty, bedraggled from travel, enters the ground floor entryway of his tenement building in New York with his luggage.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - LOWER EAST SIDE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Marty lugs his suitcase upstairs, encounters a surprised Judy exiting his apartment, ignores her greeting, and unlocks his door.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty's mom freezes at the sight of him in the kitchenette while Marty walks straight to his room avoiding eye contact.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MARTY’S ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty enters his spare bedroom with trophies and clippings on the wall, places his suitcase on the bed, notices a near-empty glass of juice on his desk, grabs it and exits.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty washes the glass and asks his mom not to eat or drink in his room, she says she only goes in to clean, he asks about his trophy from London, she says all his mail is on his bed, and he reveals he's only staying a few weeks.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BATHROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty rigorously soaps his face in the shower and a soap bubble forms in his mouth and pops.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - HALLWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty exits the bathroom in a towel and walks past nosey neighbors in the hallway who exchange awkward glances with him.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A uniformed cop arrests Marty for armed robbery in his apartment while Murray watches, revealing Murray called the cop and took money from Marty's luggage to settle a debt.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty is betrayed by his uncle Murray, who orchestrates a fake arrest to reclaim money and force repayment, escalating Marty's desperation.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty accuses the cop of being paid off by Murray, offers to pay him to perform a sex act, gets slapped, then lunges at Murray when he learns Murray stole money from his luggage.

scenes.heading: EXT. TENEMENT BUILDING - FIRE ESCAPE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty escapes by climbing down the fire escape from his bedroom window.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Murray knocks on the bedroom door, the cop rams it open to find the room empty, and sees Marty descending the fire escape below.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - LOWER EAST SIDE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty throws his duffle bag down and sprints away as a second cop in a police car chases him in reverse and crashes into a fruit stand.

scenes.heading: EXT. ESSEX STREET - LOWER EAST SIDE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty runs past Ira on the street, slows to a walk, and casually enters a pet store.

scenes.heading: INT. ESSEX STREET PETS - LOWER EAST SIDE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty enters the pet store where Rachel works, asks to use the phone, and calls Wally to create an alibi with his mother.

scenes.heading: INT. ESSEX STREET PETS - BACK ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rachel confronts Marty about being eight months pregnant with his child after leaving him many messages; Marty denies paternity, Rachel cries, and he embraces her while claiming he lost everything in London.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty learns Rachel is pregnant and claims the child is his, adding a major complication to his already chaotic life.

scenes.heading: EXT. COMMUNAL BACKYARDS/ALLEYWAYS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty flees through backyards and alleys, jumps into garbage bags to hide, and conceals himself as a cop searches above while Chinese cooks nearby either don't know or pretend not to know where he went.

scenes.heading: INT. HALSEY HOTEL - LOBBY - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty waits in a hotel lobby, meets Wally who's dressed as a cabbie, and asks him for four dollars to rent a room.

scenes.heading: INT. HALSEY HOTEL - ROOM 503 - BATHROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty runs bathwater and tells Wally there's no way the baby is his.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wally reclines in a chair flipping through Marty's mail and teases him about becoming a father.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty gets into the bathtub and asks Wally why he mentioned the Ritz.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wally tells Marty he has a bill from the IATT for fifteen hundred dollars stating his World Championship application has been denied until the balance is paid.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty bolts upright in the tub demanding to see the letter, causing the floorboards to give way.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 403 - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The bathtub crashes through the floor into the bathroom below, pinning Mishkin's arm and shoulder under it while his dog is trapped underneath; Marty is thrown to the floor and Wally calls for an ambulance.

scenes.heading: INT. 5TH FLOOR HALLWAY - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty rushes down the hallway wrapped in a towel and enters his room, telling Wally he's getting their money back.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty grabs his glasses, packs his luggage in a hurry, gets dressed, and insists on getting their money back despite Wally's protests.

scenes.heading: INT. 4TH FLOOR - HALLWAY - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty and Wally exit the elevator into a flooded hallway where hotel guests are trying to stop water from entering their rooms; Marty tells Wally to wait while he enters the damaged room.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 403 - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty confronts the desk clerk about not being warned about the bath, argues he could have broken his neck, and gets into a shouting match while workers try to fix the broken pipes.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI CAB - LINCOLN TUNNEL - SOON
scenes.summary: Wally drives through the Lincoln Tunnel with a wheezing dog in the back seat while Marty pours bourbon onto ice cream and feeds it to the dog, insisting they can win a hundred dollars.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - CRANBURY, NEW JERSEY - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty enters a crowded bowling alley and approaches Wally, who has toilet paper stuffed in both nostrils and is playing ping pong against Terry.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - TABLE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty watches Wally lose a point to Terry, then volunteers to play next and approaches an adjacent table to pick up a paddle.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLER O - TABLE 3 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Roger demands a dollar per game, Marty agrees, Roger serves and scores while Marty misses, and Terry wins his game against Wally.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - TABLE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wally tries to pay Terry after losing but Terry refuses the money; Wally tells a stranger he's a cab driver who was jumped by a fare, fought back, and took the fare's money.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - LANE 5 - LATER
scenes.summary: Roger bowls a strike, sees a crowd gathered around Marty and Wally's game, and leaves his lane to investigate.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - TABLE 3 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty beats Wally at match point and takes all his money despite Wally's pleas to keep gas money; Clark calls Roger over to play Marty next.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI CAB - CRANBURY, NJ - LATER
scenes.summary: Wally drives while Marty counts their winnings of over three hundred dollars; Wally hops out of the moving cab to dance in the road while the vehicle rolls without a driver.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNRAY GAS - SERVICE STATION
scenes.summary: A cinderblock smashes through a plate glass door at a gas station.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNWAY GAS - SERVICE STATION
scenes.summary: A hand flips a red toggle switch marked pumps at the gas station.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNRAY GAS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty fuels the cab while Wally counts four hundred thirty dollars total; a pickup truck full of men from the bowling alley skids into the station and Roger attacks Marty with a club.

scenes.heading: EXT. POTATO FIELD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Moses the dog runs through a potato field at night, breathing hard and kicking up dust.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAWRENCE’S - 53RD AND BROADWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: The damaged cab pulls up in front of Lawrence's; Marty apologizes but Wally angrily tells him to close the door and drives off.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE'S PING PONG PARLOR - STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty walks up a narrow staircase into the ping pong parlor.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE'S PING PONG PARLOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty enters the crowded parlor and is greeted with shocked stares from people who haven't seen him since his loss in England; he asks for Lawrence and is told he's in the back.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty enters the back office and finds Rachel with a suitcase and a black eye; she explains someone yelled at her, pushed her, and grabbed her shirt, and Marty embraces her.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MARTY’S ROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty quietly enters his bedroom through the fire escape, sees his mother sleeping in his bed, grabs a suit, suitcase, and a table tennis trophy, then exits.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty's mother softly calls out as he moves through the apartment and quickly leaves through the front door.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - HALLWAY AND STAIRS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty pounds on Ira's door, and when Ira opens it, Marty immediately bashes his face with the base of the trophy and leaves.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY CAR - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty and Rachel sit together on a sparsely populated subway train with their luggage, his arm around her and her head in his lap.

scenes.heading: INT. DION’S APARTMENT - PETER COOPER VILLAGE - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty brings Rachel to Dion's apartment, introduces her as his sister who needs to crash for one night due to flooding, and Dion reluctantly agrees despite not being allowed to have guests.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty excitedly discovers boxes of orange Marty Supreme ping pong balls and sees Dion's hand-drawn packaging mockup; Dion explains his father is angry about the money invested in Marty.

scenes.heading: INT. DION’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Marty calls Milton Rockwell's office and learns he's working off-site at the Morosco Theater until noon.

scenes.heading: EXT. THEATER DISTRICT - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty walks to the Morosco Theater, finds the main doors locked, and enters through a service door when a worker exits.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty walks down a long rugged hallway and enters a door on his right.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - BACKSTAGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty enters backstage, watches Kay rehearse a scene where a son stabs a suitor, and observes Milton in the audience criticizing the production manager about budget overruns.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - AUDITORIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty follows Milton and the production manager down a hallway, listening as Milton berates the manager for not controlling costs and threatens to fire him.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOROSCO THEATER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty tells Milton he's reconsidered the Japan trip and wants to do it for fifteen hundred dollars, but Milton refuses, calling him a liar and saying he won't be a resource for Marty's desperation.

scenes.heading: INT. EDISON HOTEL - BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty and Kay have sex in a hotel shower; he bites her tongue, turns her around, and secretly unclasps her expensive necklace, letting it slide down the drain.

scenes.heading: INT. EDISON HOTEL - BEDROOM - SOON
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. EDISON HOTEL - BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty stands in a hotel bathroom doorframe on the phone, assuring someone that a cashier's check for a fine is in the mail and that he will attend the Championship, while a custodian uses a plumber's snake on the shower drain.

scenes.heading: INT. UNCLE SAM’S PAWN SHOP - MIDTOWN - SOON
scenes.summary: A pawn shop proprietor examines the necklace Marty brought and tells him it's costume jewelry worth only two dollars, causing Marty to bury his head in his hands in frustration.

scenes.heading: EXT. CRANBURY NEW JERSEY - LATE AFTEROON
scenes.summary: Marty drives the Galanis' Kaiser with Rachel in the passenger seat through rural New Jersey while on the phone with Dion, instructing Rachel to retrieve a dog collar and car keys, as they approach a gas station with an 'OUT OF SERVICE' sign.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty asks Rachel what someone said and she replies 'Nothing,' then Marty notices a clapboard house in the distance and suggests they try that place.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CRANBURY - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty drives down a gravel driveway to a farmhouse, knocks on the door with no answer except barking, then follows the sound to the back and peers through a window where he sees Moses barking aggressively at him.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Marty tells Rachel he found the dog and plans to break into the house to retrieve it, instructing her to honk twice if anyone arrives, then heads to a side porch while Rachel waits in the car.

scenes.heading: INT. HOFF’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty enters the disgusting hoarder's house where Moses barks angrily at him, and when Marty approaches trying to calm the dog, Moses lunges to bite and Marty barely pulls his hand back in time.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: A beat-up pickup truck pulls into the driveway blocking the Kaiser, and when the farmer Hoff steps out, Rachel honks the horn and tells him they're looking for a lost dog.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty runs over claiming the dog is his, but Hoff denies having any dog and accuses Marty of trespassing; when Marty insists he saw the dog through the window, Hoff claims it's his dog of eight years, threatens to call the police, then retrieves a shotgun from his truck.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rachel slides to the driver's seat, guns the engine toward Marty, and plows into Hoff who rolls off the windshield cracking it and falls to the ground.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rachel commits a violent act by hitting Hoff with the car, escalating the situation to potential criminal consequences and physical harm.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The car crashes into the front porch splintering stairs and knocking over a support beam while Marty dashes into the passenger side as a dazed Hoff scrambles for the shotgun.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - CONTINIOUS
scenes.summary: Marty yells at Rachel to floor it as the car races down the driveway, Hoff fires the shotgun shattering the rear window, the car drives into a cornfield with stalks hammering the metal, and Rachel turns to Marty with a crazed, electric smile.

scenes.heading: EXT. USED CAR LOT - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty and Rachel are crammed in a telephone booth at night where Rachel calls Mishkin claiming she found his dog Moses in New Jersey, and when Mishkin asks for her address to pick up the dog, Rachel asks about a customary reward.

scenes.heading: INT. DION’S APARTMENT - PETER COOPER VILLAGE - LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty and Rachel quietly enter Dion's apartment late at night and are intercepted by an angry Dion who whispers that his parents are sleeping; Dion accuses Rachel of tricking him and is furious about his father's missing car, revealing he had to pretend to be sick to avoid picking up his parents from the airport.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty and Rachel gather their things while Dion watches from the doorway; Marty accuses Dion of being privileged and Rachel bursts into tears saying they have nowhere to go, prompting Marty to guilt Dion about throwing a pregnant woman out, then Marty asks to speak with Dion privately in the hall.

scenes.heading: EXT. PETER COOPER VILLAGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A thousand ping pong balls rain down onto the street from Peter Cooper Village.

scenes.heading: INT. PETER COOPER VILLAGE - ELEVATOR - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty and Rachel ride the elevator down in silence and step out into the lobby.

scenes.heading: INT. PETER COOPER VILLAGE - LOBBY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty tells Rachel he has a purpose and she doesn't, explaining that his obligation to see something specific through requires sacrifice and forces difficult choices, while her life is just making it up as she goes along, and he states this is not how he am.

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S APARTMENT - LOWER EAST SIDE - AFTER
scenes.summary: Rachel enters her dark apartment, approaches Ira who is asleep on the bed with a bruised and swollen face, wakes him, and tells him it's not his baby.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rachel reveals to Ira that the baby isn't his, a devastating truth that will irreversibly alter their relationship and trigger major consequences.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty plays table tennis with great ferocity, executing smash after smash after smash.

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ira is in a blind rage, pulling down a shelf and throwing a sewing machine that blows a hole in the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty, drenched in sweat, practices his defense with relentless mechanized undercuts as the camera zooms into an extreme close-up of his eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S APARTMENT - DAWN
scenes.summary: Ira rages and dumps women's clothing into luggage, then carries it to the front door where concerned neighbors including Judy and Marty's mom are waiting; Ira sarcastically calls out to Rachel about her new mother-in-law and congratulates Marty's mom.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - DAWN
scenes.summary: Lawrence leaves and Marty flips off the lights one at a time, then makes his way in darkness to Lawrence's office where he plops down on the couch and stares at a large standee of himself that says 'Marty Mauser -- the pride of Lawrences.'

scenes.heading: EXT. SEWARD PARK - SOON AFTER
scenes.summary: Rachel meets Mishkin and Mitch in Seward Park; Mishkin has his arm in a cast and sling, and when Rachel asks for the money before showing the dog, Mishkin refuses and threatens her, but Rachel shows him the collar and says the dog is across the street, then Mishkin sends Mitch to verify while keeping Rachel with him.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDGE OF PARK - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Mishkin races toward the dog held by a bartender, realizes immediately it's not Moses, grabs the bartender by the throat demanding answers; the bartender drops the leash and the dog runs into traffic, then the bartender punches Mishkin who pulls out a knife and stabs him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mishkin stabs the bartender after discovering the dog scam, marking a violent escalation that will have severe criminal and narrative consequences.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - DRESSING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty arrives at Kay's dressing room asking her to lunch, but she coldly refuses saying they open tonight; Marty steps in and nervously pulls out the necklace he stole from her, confessing he planned to sell it but can't stop thinking about her, but Kay laughs snidely and reveals it's costume jewelry worth nothing.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - BOX OFFICE - SHOWTIME
scenes.summary: Marty receives an envelope at the box office containing a single ticket and a note from Kay asking him to meet her in Central Park at midnight.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty is seated near the back of the theater among Rockwell employees when he notices Fred Astaire being led to his seat, then sees Milton walking right behind Astaire and watches Milton take his seat as the curtain rises.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTRAL PARK - JUST PAST MIDNIGHT
scenes.summary: Kay approaches Marty in Central Park just past midnight, pulls him into a dark archway tunnel, kisses him, then pulls out an expensive diamond necklace and clasps it around his neck, explaining her husband gives her jewelry every year and she has twenty-five pieces representing years of misery.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREAT LAWN - SOON
scenes.summary: Kay and Marty fall onto the grass kissing and she pushes his head between her legs; a patrolman shines a flashlight over them and orders them to stand up, but on Marty's count of three they spring up and run, only to be caught by a second patrolman who orders them to put their hands where he can see them.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIFTH AVENUE - SOON
scenes.summary: Kay and Marty rush down Fifth Avenue without the necklace, and Marty asks Kay to grab him another one; Kay refuses saying she's been gone too long and her mother is upstairs, but after Marty pleads that she has twenty-five of them, Kay reluctantly agrees and tells him to wait outside and not come up.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Kay passes through the staff quarters into the packed party where Milton approaches annoyed asking where she's been; he tells her everyone's looking for her and that Merle is on the phone with the New York Times waiting to hear the review, then Merle waves Kay over excitedly.

scenes.heading: EXT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty grows impatient waiting outside, paces in front of the building, then blends in with a pack of arriving partygoers and enters the lobby.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - 2ND FLOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty ascends the marble stairwell with a group but encounters exiting partygoers who say the party's over; Marty continues up past them to the next landing where the party is thinning out with a muted vibe, and he cautiously wanders down a hall past a band packing up.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - 3RD FLOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty hears muffled voices behind a closed door, knocks, and asks a high society woman if Kay is there, but she says it's not a good time and closes the door; Marty catches a glimpse of Kay crying hysterically before the door shuts, leaving him in utter desolation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty discovers Kay is devastated (likely from a bad review), marking the collapse of his romantic pursuit and his hopes tied to her world.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - 2RD FLOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty walks into the den where Milton is seated with well-groomed men in a jovial mood; Marty apologizes and begs Milton on his hands and knees for another chance, asking for a ride to Japan and fifteen hundred dollars to compete in the Championship, promising to work under Milton and even lose if needed, but Milton refuses saying there are no second chances and tells Marty to leave.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - STUDY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty enters Milton's study and picks up a sponge racket with the Rockwell Ink logo screen-printed on it.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - DEN - MOMENTS LATER 168A 168A
scenes.summary: Marty returns to the den with the paddle and hands it to Milton, who then tells Marty to bend over a chair and drop his pants for a spanking; when Marty hesitates, Milton confirms he'll bring Marty to Japan and compensate him if he complies, revealing they leave from LaGuardia at 8am on Milton's private plane.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty agrees to be humiliated and spanked by Milton in exchange for getting to Japan, a devastating moment of self-degradation that secures his trip but at enormous personal cost.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: A numb Marty leans against the subway car window as light flits past him and he sinks deeper into dejection.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - LATERN
scenes.summary: Marty enters Lawrence's parlor and asks to crash in the office because he has a flight to Japan at 8am; Lawrence is excited about the trip but Marty seems unenthusiastic, then Lawrence mentions Wally is looking for him and Marty lights up scanning the room, but is suddenly intercepted by Mishkin and Mitch.

scenes.heading: INT. NASH AMBASSADOR - CRANBURY, NJ - LATER
scenes.summary: Mishkin's car drives along a dark rural road with Reuben driving, Mishkin in the passenger seat, and Marty and Rachel crammed in back with Mitch in a quiet, tense mood; Marty directs them to turn left after the gas station toward the farmhouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. NASH AMBASSADOR - CRANBURY, NJ - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. NASH AMBASSADOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. BELLEVUE HOSPITAL - ER - LATER
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. HANEDA AIRPORT - JAPAN - MORNING
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. JAPANESE COUNTRYSIDE - SOON
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - LEFT OF STAGE
scenes.summary: Marty waits in line at the amphitheater disguised in a hat, watching challengers lose to Endo while press and military officials gather nearby beneath a humiliating mural of his London defeat.

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - CONCESSION STANDS - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty approaches Sethi at the concession stands to apologize and explain he's only participating to pay his fine, but Sethi coldly cuts him off and walks away before he can finish.

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - BY THE STAGE - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty removes his hat on stage and dramatically reveals his identity to the crowd, announcing himself as the challenger to Endo while the audience recognizes him and reacts with excitement.

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty performs as the heel during his match with Endo, deliberately losing points through theatrical mistakes like pretending to trip on a cable, and Endo wins the first game 20-14 to crowd applause.

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: With the score tied 17-17 and the amphitheater packed, Marty takes the lead 18-17, prompting Milton to approach and threaten to expose Marty's lies to Rachel unless he throws the match.
scenes.turningPointNote: Milton leverages Marty's deepest vulnerability—his lies to Rachel—forcing an impossible choice between winning and protecting his relationship, crystallizing the consequences of his deception.

scenes.heading: EXT. YOKOTA AIR BASE - AIRFIELD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty rushes through a military airbase in pouring rain and boards a transport plane after learning over the phone that Rachel was rushed to the maternity floor weeks early.

scenes.heading: INT. DC-4 AIRPLANE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty sits aboard the plane surrounded by jubilant soldiers returning home while he frantically argues with the operator on the phone about Rachel's condition and demands to be connected to the maternity ward.

scenes.heading: INT. STEWART AIR NATIONAL GUARD BASE - HUDSON, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty walks briskly through the terminal past soldiers reuniting with their families.

scenes.heading: INT. BELLEVUE HOSPITAL - MATERNITY WARD - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty runs past the nurse's station into the maternity ward, sees his mother and Rachel's family in the waiting area looking unhappy to see him, and bursts into room 613.

scenes.heading: INT. MATERNITY WARD - ROOM 613 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty finds Rachel asleep in a crowded recovery room, kisses her forehead and promises he's not going anywhere, but she closes her eyes again without responding.

scenes.heading: INT. MATERNITY WARD - NURSERY - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty stares at his newborn baby through the nursery window and suddenly breaks down sobbing uncontrollably as the baby stretches and cries.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty's emotional collapse at seeing his child marks the culmination of his journey—confronted with the reality of fatherhood and the weight of all his choices, he experiences a complete psychological breaking point.
